title: (SACRAMENTO, CA)

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 260 aims to protect access to medication abortion in California as other states rollback reproductive healthcare access.",
    "Mifepristone is a safe, FDA-approved medication for over 20 years.",
    "AB 260 offers comprehensive protections for healthcare providers, pharmacists, clinics, and hospitals from penalties for legally prescribing or dispensing mifepristone.",
    "AB 260 mandates insurance coverage, including Medi-Cal, for medication abortion.",
    "The bill expands access to medication abortion through telehealth services.",
    "AB 260 eliminates outdated and discriminatory language from state law.",
    "Supporters of AB 260 highlight that it strengthens legal protections and modernizes access to reproductive care.",
    "AB 260 ensures equitable care for marginalized communities and Medi-Cal recipients who face greater barriers to healthcare.",
    "Abortion medication is both safe and effective.",
    "AB 260 establishes clear protections for providers, pharmacies, and clinics that legally dispense mifepristone.",
    "AB 260 supports access through telehealth.",
    "The bill protects provider privacy by removing names from prescription labels.",
    "AB 260 is designed to safeguard medication abortion amidst federal threats to mifepristone's accessibility.",
    "The National Health Law Program states that AB 260 will ensure Medi-Cal coverage of asynchronous telehealth for abortion services.",
    "AB 260 addresses access barriers associated with synchronous telehealth that may disproportionately harm Medi-Cal enrollees.",
    "The bill represents a crucial advancement in protecting and expanding abortion access in California."
  ]
}
```
